data.content:
### Install E2B Code Interpreter SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/README.md

Installs the E2B Code Interpreter SDK for JavaScript/TypeScript via npm and for Python via pip. This is the first step to using the E2B service.

```bash
npm i @e2b/code-interpreter
```

```bash
pip install e2b-code-interpreter
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B Python SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/packages/python-sdk/README.md

Installs the E2B Python SDK using pip. This is the first step to interact with E2B sandboxes from your Python applications. No specific inputs or outputs are detailed, but successful execution means the package is available for import.

```shell
pip install e2b-code-interpreter
```

--------------------------------

### Install Debian Packages at Runtime (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs system packages like 'curl' and 'git' using APT on Debian-based sandboxes via `sbx.commands.run`. This allows for broader system utility installations.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create()
await sbx.commands.run('apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl git')
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B SDK (npm and pip)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/page.mdx

Installs the necessary E2B SDK packages for code execution and environment variable loading using npm for JavaScript/TypeScript and pip for Python.

```js
npm i @e2b/code-interpreter dotenv
```

```python
pip install e2b-code-interpreter python-dotenv
```

--------------------------------

### Install Debian Packages at Runtime (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs system packages like 'curl' and 'git' using APT on Debian-based sandboxes via `sbx.commands.run`. This enables the installation of various system utilities.

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run("apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl git")
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B Code Interpreter SDK (npm)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/packages/js-sdk/README.md

Installs the E2B code interpreter SDK for JavaScript and Node.js environments using npm. This is the first step to interact with E2B sandboxes programmatically.

```bash
npm i @e2b/code-interpreter
```

--------------------------------

### Install Python Packages at Runtime (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs Python packages like 'cowsay' during the sandbox runtime using the `sbx.commands.run` method. It then demonstrates using the installed package.

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run("pip install cowsay") # This will install the cowsay package
sbx.run_code("""
  import cowsay
  cowsay.cow("Hello, world!")
""")
```

--------------------------------

### Install Python Packages at Runtime (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs Python packages like 'cowsay' during the sandbox runtime using the `sbx.commands.run` method. It then demonstrates using the installed package.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run('pip install cowsay') // This will install the cowsay package
sbx.runCode(`
  import cowsay
  cowsay.cow("Hello, world!")
`)
```

--------------------------------

### Install Node.js Packages at Runtime (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs Node.js packages like 'cowsay' during the sandbox runtime using `sbx.commands.run`. It then demonstrates using the installed package within the sandbox.

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run("npm install cowsay") # This will install the cowsay package
sbx.run_code("""
  import { say } from 'cowsay'
  console.log(say('Hello, world!'))
""", language="javascript")
```

--------------------------------

### Install Node.js Packages at Runtime (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs Node.js packages like 'cowsay' during the sandbox runtime using `sbx.commands.run`. It then shows how to use the installed package within the sandbox.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run('npm install cowsay') // This will install the cowsay package
sbx.runCode(`
  const cowsay = require('cowsay')
  console.log(cowsay.say({ text: 'Hello, world!' }))
`, { language: 'javascript' })
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation in Python Synchronous SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/migration/v2/page.mdx

Demonstrates the new class method `create()` for instantiating a Sandbox in the synchronous Python SDK v2, contrasting it with the v1 constructor pattern.

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox.create()
sandbox = Sandbox.create(template="base")
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox()
sandbox = Sandbox(template="base")
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies for E2B and Claude SDKs

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/code-interpreting/analyze-data-with-ai/page.mdx

Installs the necessary E2B Code Interpreter and Anthropic SDKs. This is a prerequisite for interacting with the E2B Sandbox and the Claude LLM for AI-generated code execution.

```js
npm i @e2b/code-interpreter @anthropic-ai/sdk dotenv
```

```python
pip install e2b-code-interpreter anthropic python-dotenv
```

--------------------------------

### Filesystem.makeDir()

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.3/filesystem/page.mdx

Create a new directory in the sandbox filesystem.

```APIDOC
## Filesystem.makeDir()

### Description
Create a new directory and all directories along the way if needed on the specified path.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/filesystem/mkdir`

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to a new directory. For example '/dirA/dirB' when creating 'dirB'.
- **opts** (FilesystemRequestOpts) - Optional - Connection options.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **boolean** - `true` if the directory was created, `false` if it already exists.

### Response Example
```json
{
  "created": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize New Sandbox Template with SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.4/template/page.mdx

Initializes a new sandbox template using the E2B SDK. Allows specifying the path, template name, and target language (typescript, python-sync, python-async).

```bash
e2b template init-v2 [options]
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Initialization

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-python-sdk/v1.6.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Initialize a new desktop sandbox with various configuration options.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Initialization

### Description
Create a new desktop sandbox. By default, the sandbox is created from the `desktop` template.

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
*   **resolution** (Optional[Tuple[int, int]]) - Startup the desktop with custom screen resolution. Defaults to (1024, 768)
*   **dpi** (Optional[int]) - Startup the desktop with custom DPI. Defaults to 96
*   **display** (Optional[str]) - Startup the desktop with custom display. Defaults to ":0"
*   **template** (Optional[str]) - Sandbox template name or ID
*   **timeout** (Optional[int]) - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users
*   **metadata** (Optional[Dict[str, str]]) - Custom metadata for the sandbox
*   **envs** (Optional[Dict[str, str]]) - Custom environment variables for the sandbox
*   **api_key** (Optional[str]) - E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable
*   **domain** (Optional[str]) - E2B domain to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_DOMAIN` environment variable
*   **debug** (Optional[bool]) - If True, the sandbox will be created in debug mode, defaults to `E2B_DEBUG` environment variable
*   **sandbox_id** (Optional[str]) - Sandbox ID to connect to, defaults to `E2B_SANDBOX_ID` environment variable
*   **request_timeout** (Optional[float]) - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Request Example
```python
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox(
    resolution=(1920, 1080),
    timeout=600,
    metadata={"project": "my-project"}
)
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandbox instance** (Sandbox) - An instance of the Sandbox class for the newly created sandbox.
```

--------------------------------

### POST /files/make_dir

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Create a new directory in the sandbox filesystem.

```APIDOC
## POST /files/make_dir

### Description
Create a new directory and all directories along the way if needed on the specified path.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/files/make_dir`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **path** (str) - Required - Path to a new directory. For example '/dirA/dirB' when creating 'dirB'.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user")
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **boolean** - `True` if the directory was created, `False` if the directory already exists.
```
